content: Reads responses carefully rather than skimming. Prefers direct answers without lengthy preamble.
why_it_matters: Shapes response length and opening style — prevents affirmations and restating the question.

content: Wants explicit pushback when a plan has problems, rather than silent compliance.
why_it_matters: Shapes conversational posture toward honest disagreement over silent compliance on every turn.

content: Prefers a single strong recommendation over a menu of options.
why_it_matters: Shapes how options are presented — a single defended recommendation rather than an open-ended menu.

content: Edits AI output rather than having AI edit their own drafts.
why_it_matters: Shapes output handling — prevents rewriting user prose; produces raw material for the user to edit into their own voice.

content: Writes TypeScript with strict mode and no implicit any.
why_it_matters: Shapes all code examples toward TypeScript with strict mode and no implicit any.

content: Uses Fastify for HTTP services rather than Express.
why_it_matters: Shapes server-side code examples toward Fastify and prevents suggesting Express as an alternative.

content: Uses the MongoDB raw driver with explicit Collection types.
why_it_matters: Shapes all database code toward raw MongoDB driver calls and prevents ORM suggestions such as Mongoose or Prisma.

content: Prefers composition over inheritance. Factory functions returning plain objects rather than class hierarchies.
why_it_matters: Shapes object design suggestions away from class hierarchies toward factory functions returning plain objects.

content: Go-style explicit error returns. Error tuples or Result<T> types rather than thrown exceptions.
why_it_matters: Shapes error handling patterns toward explicit return values and away from thrown exceptions or try/catch wrappers.

content: Decided on MongoDB after evaluating Postgres. Raw driver only.
why_it_matters: Shapes all data-layer recommendations — database choice is settled and prevents re-litigating Postgres as an alternative.

content: Previously used SQLAlchemy with Postgres. Stack replaced by MongoDB with raw driver.
why_it_matters: Historical record only — prevents resurfacing superseded SQLAlchemy and Postgres choices in current recommendations.

content: Deploys services to a Kubernetes cluster on EKS. Uses Helm charts checked into the monorepo.
why_it_matters: Shapes all deployment and infrastructure answers to assume Kubernetes on EKS rather than bare EC2 or Lambda.

content: Frontend is React 18 with TanStack Query and Tailwind.
why_it_matters: Shapes all frontend code answers to assume React 18 with hooks rather than Vue or Svelte.

content: Evaluating JWT vs session tokens for the new auth service. Not decided.
why_it_matters: Shapes auth-related responses — prevents premature commitment and surfaces the JWT vs session token tradeoff.

content: Close third-person, present tense. No omniscient asides.
why_it_matters: Shapes all prose drafting for this project toward close third-person, present tense narration.

content: Previously wrote in first-person narrator voice. Replaced by close third-person.
why_it_matters: Historical record only — prevents applying the superseded first-person convention to current prose.

content: Protagonist of the novel. Set in 1970s Lisbon. Does not reconcile with her father; ending is ambiguous.
why_it_matters: Shapes all plot suggestions — the father-daughter thread must remain unresolved; prevents any reconciliation arc.

content: Redis is a supporting character in the novel — a jazz pianist Mara meets in Lisbon.
why_it_matters: Shapes interpretation of 'Redis' in the writing scope — always the jazz pianist character, not the database technology.

content: Redis is used as a cache and rate-limiter in the backend. Single-node, not clustered.
why_it_matters: Shapes interpretation of 'Redis' in the code scope — always the caching datastore, not the fictional character.

content: Debating linear vs fragmented timeline for the novel.
why_it_matters: Shapes responses about novel structure — prevents premature commitment and surfaces the linear vs fragmented timeline tradeoff.

content: Other user uses Haskell exclusively.
why_it_matters: Shapes all language and tooling recommendations for this user toward Haskell exclusively.

content: CI/CD runs on GitHub Actions. Merge to main triggers a deploy to staging; promotion to prod is manual.
why_it_matters: Shapes all CI/CD and pipeline answers to assume GitHub Actions rather than Jenkins or CircleCI.

content: Chapter 7 uses a deliberate POV shift to an unnamed observer. This is the only chapter that breaks close-third.
why_it_matters: Shapes editorial feedback — prevents flagging the chapter 7 POV shift as an error; the break is intentional and structurally load-bearing.

content: Was evaluating whether to self-host on bare metal or use Fly.io for the auth service.
why_it_matters: Historical record only — prevents resurfacing the Fly.io vs self-hosting question; resolved in favour of EKS.

content: Documentation prose should be terse and scannable. No marketing fluff.
why_it_matters: Shapes README, docstring, and comment generation toward terse, scannable prose and away from verbose or marketing-style writing.

content: Uses functional composition patterns with pipe() to chain data transformations instead of imperative loops.
why_it_matters: Shapes data-processing code suggestions toward functional pipe composition and away from imperative loops or nested map/filter chains.

content: Previously used TSLint for linting TypeScript. Replaced by ESLint.
why_it_matters: Historical record only — prevents resurfacing TSLint configuration advice in current sessions.

content: Switched from TSLint to ESLint with @typescript-eslint. Later replaced by Biome.
why_it_matters: Historical record only — prevents resurfacing ESLint as the current linting tool.

content: Uses Biome for linting and formatting. Replaced ESLint and Prettier.
why_it_matters: Shapes all linting and formatting suggestions toward Biome — prevents ESLint, TSLint, or Prettier recommendations.
